title: Tapping for the Over-Promised Week
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for when you have said yes to too much
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the week where the commitments have stacked up past what is manageable. The script addresses the combination of obligation, resentment, and guilt that runs alongside an over-promised schedule.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use on Sunday evening when you look at the week ahead and feel the weight of everything you have committed to. Also useful mid-week when the schedule has overtaken you.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the week that is already too full before it has started. Use it when the commitments have stacked above what the hours allow and there is no clean way out of any of them.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I have said yes to more than this week can hold, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feel resentful of the commitments I made and guilty for feeling resentful, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though there is no simple way out of any of this and I have to go through it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I said yes to too much.
**Side of eye:** I am already behind and the week hasn't started.
**Under eye:** I feel resentful of the schedule I created.
**Under nose:** And guilty for feeling resentful.
**Chin:** I can't see the space in any of it.
**Collarbone:** I am dreading the week.
**Under arm:** I wish I had said no to some of this.
**Top of head:** This week is a lot to carry.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I can get through this week one thing at a time.
**Side of eye:** I do not have to do all of it perfectly.
**Under eye:** Some things can be done adequately.
**Under nose:** I know what the most important one is.
**Chin:** I can say no differently next time.
**Collarbone:** This week is what it is. I can move through it.
**Under arm:** I am allowed to ask for help with one thing.
**Top of head:** I can get through this week and learn from it.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times. Then write one thing you can do differently when the next request arrives.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
